Stucco Repair in Hays County, TX

Hays County stretches from the booming suburbs south of Austin through the scenic Hill Country around Wimberley and Dripping Springs. That range of terrain brings different stucco challenges depending on where your home sits, and Star Stucco of Austin has the experience to handle all of them — from clay-soil cracking in Kyle to UV damage on hilltop properties near Dripping Springs.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

From Blackland Prairie to Hill Country: Stucco in Hays County

Hays County is split by a geological boundary that directly affects how stucco performs. The eastern half — including Kyle, Buda, and San Marcos — sits on the Blackland Prairie with deep, expansive clay soils that swell when wet and shrink when dry. This soil movement is a leading cause of foundation shifting and the stucco cracks that follow.

The western half, including Dripping Springs and Wimberley, sits on the Edwards Plateau where limestone bedrock and thinner soils create a more stable foundation but expose stucco to even more intense sun and wind. Homes in this area tend to experience UV-driven surface degradation and cracking from thermal cycling rather than soil-related movement.

No matter which side of the county your property is on, our repair approach starts with understanding the local conditions. We do not apply the same fix to a clay-soil crack in Buda that we would to a sun-damaged wall in Wimberley, because the underlying causes are different and the repair materials need to match.

What's Included With Our Stucco Repair in Hays County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

Detailed Damage Assessment

We walk your property and document every area of concern, from obvious cracks to subtle signs of moisture movement or surface degradation.

Subsurface Moisture Testing

We probe behind the stucco at damaged and suspect areas to determine if water has reached the sheathing or framing.

Damaged Material Removal

All compromised stucco is removed cleanly back to sound material, giving the repair a proper surface to bond against.

Substrate & Lath Repair

Any deteriorated building paper, wire lath, or sheathing is replaced before the new stucco is applied.

Color & Texture Matching

We custom-blend the repair material to match both the color and the surface texture of your existing stucco.

Sealed Finish Application

The completed repair is sealed against moisture and UV to extend the life of the fix in the Hays County climate.

Common Stucco Repair Needs in Hays County

The stucco issues we see in Hays County vary by location, but these are the most common problems we repair throughout the area.

Cracking Around Openings

Windows and doors concentrate stress from foundation movement and thermal expansion. We remove the failed stucco, reinforce the area, and apply a flexible repair that accommodates ongoing movement.

Water Damage Restoration

The heavy spring storms that roll through Hays County push water behind stucco through even small cracks. We trace the moisture path, dry the wall, and rebuild with proper waterproofing.

Patch Failure Repair

Quick patches applied without addressing the underlying cause fall off within a season or two. We do the prep work and substrate repair that makes the next fix the last one.

Delamination

Stucco that has separated from the lath creates hollow, unstable areas. We remove the delaminated sections and rebuild with better mechanical bonding.

Efflorescence & Mineral Staining

White crystalline deposits on stucco indicate moisture is moving through the wall. We eliminate the moisture source and clean the surface to restore its appearance.

Impact & Hail Damage

Spring hail and windblown debris cause chips, dents, and surface fractures. We rebuild the damaged area and match the original finish.

Why Choose Us for Stucco Repair in Hays County?

  • We identify and address the root cause of every repair, whether that is soil movement, moisture intrusion, or UV breakdown.
  • Our repair materials are selected based on your property's specific conditions — clay-soil areas get different treatment than Hill Country limestone sites.
  • Color and texture matching is part of every job, ensuring the repair looks like part of the original wall.
  • We resolve hidden substrate and moisture issues before applying new stucco, which is why our repairs last.
  • All repairs are backed by a written warranty on materials and workmanship.
  • Free inspections across Hays County — call (512) 706-9699 to schedule.

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Hays County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our Stucco Repair Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Schedule an Inspection

Call or reach out online to set up a free visit. We serve Hays County from San Marcos and Kyle to Dripping Springs and Wimberley.

2

Diagnose the Issue

Our technician inspects the damage, tests for moisture, and determines the root cause — soil movement, water intrusion, UV degradation, or a combination.

3

Review the Repair Plan

You receive a written proposal with a clear description of the work and an itemized cost. No pressure — take the time you need to decide.

4

We Handle the Repair

Our crew removes damaged material, fixes the substrate, and applies a color-matched, sealed finish. We clean the work area and walk you through the completed repair.
